priors.text: Great human reviewers check: intent alignment, design fit, blast radius, completeness -- not just correctness
priors.notes: Google guide (9 dimensions including design first), Gergely Orosz (system context), Sphinx (5-domain decomposition) all corroborate this

priors.text: Mediocre reviewers focus on style and surface, miss semantic concerns
priors.notes: Chelsea Troy (delegation without running code), Sphinx (fluent but shallow), EASE 2026 implication

priors.text: Expert reviewers ask 'what is the simplest thing that could go wrong with this change in production?' and work backwards from failure
priors.notes: Not directly confirmed by fetched sources; closest match is OWASP security review mental model (SQ4-C8) but that's security-specific

priors.text: LLM reviewers fail by: pattern-matching on surface; attributing pre-existing issues to the PR; verbosity diluting real findings; lacking codebase context; missing intent alignment
priors.notes: Sphinx (limited contextual understanding, noisy supervision), EASE 2026 (context constraints), synthesis claim SQ3-C8 covers all 5 modes

priors.text: Google Engineering Practices is the canonical written reference -- 9 review dimensions
priors.notes: Directly verified: Design, Functionality, Complexity, Tests, Naming, Comments, Style, Documentation, Every Line

priors.text: Chelsea Troy has influential writing on reviewing code you don't fully understand
priors.notes: Article fetched and read; focus is on participation principle and context transfer, not 'reviewing without understanding'

priors.text: The biggest failure mode is reviewing only what changed, not the system impact of what changed
priors.notes: Gergely Orosz (system context distinction), Sphinx (context blindness), Pragmatic Engineer (diff vs. system review)

priors.text: LLMs improve with: concrete checklist, steelman-before-criticize, explicit false-positive suppression
priors.notes: Checklist is VALIDATED (Sphinx, 40% improvement). Steelman-before-criticize and false-positive suppression have NO empirical validation in fetched literature. Prior is partially falsified for the latter two claims.

priors.text: Specialization (security reviewer vs. correctness reviewer vs. performance reviewer) produces better findings than generalist
priors.notes: SOEN-101 role specialization (15% improvement), Google guide (specialist reviewers for complex concerns), Sphinx 5-domain decomposition, OWASP security specialization. Partially empirically supported.

priors.text: Dual reading technique (once for understanding, once adversarially) is taught in security training
priors.notes: Not directly confirmed or denied in fetched sources. OWASP guide covers security review methodology but did not fetch the specific dual-reading technique description.

priors.text: A reviewer who doesn't understand should ask questions, not write findings -- LLMs always write findings instead
priors.notes: Google guide explicitly states this (SQ1-C9); LLM failure mode #5 (SQ3-C8); structural guardrail 5 (SQ6-C6)

priors.text: EASE 2026 paper found only 0.44-0.62 developer agreement on LLM review findings
priors.notes: Paper VERIFIED (arXiv:2604.24525, Karakaya et al., accepted EASE 2026). Figure is real BUT: it measures LLM-as-Judge vs. developer labels, not direct developer agreement on LLM findings. The prior statement is directionally correct but the numerics need nuance.
